steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The linguist assumes 'know' appears quite often in written English. The corpus data then shows 'know' is the 8th most commonly used verb. The blank describes what the corpus data does to the assumption — the data backs it up rather than opposing it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — substantiate: To substantiate means to support or prove with evidence. The corpus data (8th most common verb) confirms the assumption that 'know' is frequent. ✓
  • Choice B — entail: To entail means to logically require or involve. An assumption does not logically require a corpus finding — that is not the relationship described. ✗
  • Choice C — transpose: To transpose means to reverse the order or position of things. The corpus data does not reverse the assumption. ✗
  • Choice D — refute: To refute means to disprove. The data supports the assumption, not contradicts it. ✗
That matches Choice A — substantiate.
